stations.station_name: EKG Warm-Up: Baseline Rhythm ID Assessment
stations.description: Baseline rhythm ID quiz — timed, scored, all 20 core rhythms. This establishes the per-rhythm starting point for every student. Score per-rhythm: Correct / Partial / Missed.
equipment: Printed rhythm strips (all 20 core rhythms) or monitor display

stations.description: Review team roles for the semester: Team Lead, Medication Tech, Monitor Tech, Airway Tech. Assign groups. Set expectations for the minicode walk-through format.

stations.station_name: ACLS Rhythm Leadership Drill (Minicode — Walk-Through Phase)
stations.description: Team-based ACLS rhythm scenarios. Walk-Through phase: student verbalizes every decision before acting. Instructor actively coaches — prompts with questions, corrects errors immediately. No time pressure. Run 2 rhythm cases per Team Lead, rotate every 8-10 min. Rhythm cases: SVT (stable/unstable), symptomatic bradycardia, VF/VT arrest, Torsades.
equipment: Cardiac monitor/defibrillator with rhythm generator or pre-programmed strips
IV/IO training equipment with med syringes or mock drug kit

instructor_tiering.three_instructors: Run 2 minicode stations simultaneously + 1 instructor managing rhythm strip review group
instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 1 runs minicode + 1 manages remaining students on rhythm strip review
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: Run minicode with full group observing; structured debrief after each Team Lead rotation

common_errors: Failing to confirm rhythm before intervention

stations.description: Key takeaways from the session. Log baseline EKG scores and minicode participation in webapp. Preview Week 3.
5 rhythm strips, timed recognition drill. Recognition only — student names the rhythm. Score per strip.
Review objectives: individual drill card stations today. Assign groups to stations. Instructors select stations from available pool.

stations.station_name: Drill Card Stations — Set A (Instructor Selects from Pool)
stations.description: Individual drill card stations. Instructors select from available station pool based on staffing and student completion needs. Students rotate through stations. Each station 8-10 min per student.
equipment: Station-specific equipment per selected drill cards

available_stations.description: Rapid decision-making: student in hot seat receives case vignette, 30-60 sec to announce impression, interventions, next steps. 14 case bank available.
Student performs psychomotor skill (IV/IO, airway, or med draw) while answering clinical reasoning questions. Must answer while continuing skill.
Team Lead assigns tasks, directs interventions, gives radio report. 7-8 min scenarios with crew members.
Manage patient with deliberately limited equipment. Instructor announces resource restrictions.
Webapp-integrated rhythm strip assessment. Standard set (8-10 rhythms). Instructor grades per-rhythm.

instructor_tiering.three_instructors: 3 stations simultaneously; students rotate every 8-10 min. Remaining students on self-directed rhythm strip packet.
instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 2 stations + 1 self-directed rhythm packet or protocol case cards. Rotate groups.
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: 1 station at a time; remaining students work rhythm strips or protocol review independently.

stations.description: Key takeaways. Log all station completions in webapp. Preview Week 4.
5 rhythm strips, 2 minutes each. Recognition focus — name the rhythm. Score per strip.
Today: team lead scenarios with cognitive stressors + minicode (walk-through). Assign groups and initial Team Leads.

Team lead scenarios with cognitive stressors (questioning, interruptions). Minicode in Walk-Through phase: student verbalizes every decision before acting. Rotate Team Lead each scenario. Run 3-4 scenarios. Scenarios: CHF exacerbation, hypoglycemia, MVC trauma. Add minicode rhythm case between scenarios.
equipment: IV/IO kit, drug kit, airway equipment

instructor_tiering.three_instructors: 1 runs scenario, 1 acts as FTO/evaluator, 1 manages rhythm warm-up group for non-active students
instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 1 runs scenario + evaluates, 1 manages remaining students on structured activity
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: Run scenario with full group observing; structured debrief after each rotation. Observers have protocol case cards.

stations.description: Key takeaways on communication and team leading. Log scenario participation and roles. Preview Week 5.
5 rhythms — student must ID rhythm AND verbalize the treatment plan before moving on. Recognition + treatment phase begins.
Drill card stations Set B + ACLS algorithm walkthrough. Minicode now enters Pressured phase — inform students. Assign groups.

stations.station_name: Drill Card Stations — Set B + ACLS Walkthrough
stations.description: Instructors select from remaining station pool. Focus on stations students haven't completed yet. Include static cardiology (12-lead) if instructor available. ACLS algorithm walkthrough can run as a group station.

instructor_tiering.three_instructors: 3 stations simultaneously. Prioritize stations with lowest completion rates.
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: 1 station at a time; remaining students work independently.

stations.description: Log completions. Review mid-point station completion status. Preview Week 6 trauma focus.

stations.station_name: EKG Warm-Up: Rhythm ID During Trauma
stations.description: 5 rhythms — student IDs rhythm AND states treatment. Context: 'Your trauma patient is on the monitor — what do you see and what do you do?'
Today: junctional wound packing + hemorrhagic shock scenario. Assign groups.

Individual junctional wound packing drill. Student receives trauma scenario card, identifies junctional hemorrhage, packs wound with hemostatic gauze, applies pressure dressing. Concurrent hemorrhagic shock management. 8-10 min per student.
common_errors: Laying gauze on top instead of packing
Tunnel vision on wound ignoring shock

stations.description: Full team scenario: trauma patient with hemorrhagic shock. Team Lead runs the call. Pressured minicode phase applies — time constraints and interruptions active. Rotate Team Lead between scenarios.
Key takeaways on hemorrhage control and shock management. Log completions and scenario participation. Preview Week 7 MCI drill.

templates.title: S3 Week 7 - MCI Drill
templates.review_notes: Requires volunteer patient coordination at least 2 weeks prior. Contingency: if volunteers fall through, repurpose as team lead scenario block. Coordinate with ALS Operations course schedule.

stations.description: Full MCI exercise with volunteer patients. Students rotate through incident commander and treatment roles. Include at least one cardiac arrest patient within the MCI for rhythm recognition integration. Debrief focuses on triage decisions, communication, and resource allocation.
equipment: Volunteer patients with moulage and scenario cards
Full field equipment for multiple patient contacts

contingency.title: S3 Week 7 - Team Lead Scenario Block (MCI Contingency)
contingency.description: If MCI volunteers are unavailable, run team lead scenarios with Pressured phase minicode.

stations.description: Entire session is ACLS-focused. Warm-up: 5 rhythms with full treatment pathway verbalization including drug doses.
Today: full megacode practice + Pressured phase minicode. Time pressure and interruptions active. Assign rotation order.

Individual megacode rotations. Each student runs at least one complete megacode as Team Lead. Pressured phase: time constraints, interruptions (bystander questions, equipment issues), questioning during task. Instructor intervenes only on critical safety errors.
equipment: Mock drug kit (epi, amiodarone, atropine, adenosine, mag sulfate)

instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 1 megacode station + 1 algorithm walkthrough/rhythm review station
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: 1 megacode at a time; group observes and debriefs between rotations

stations.description: Debrief megacode performance. Review mid-semester completion status from webapp. Identify students with gaps. Determine Week 9 flex content based on data. Log completions.

templates.title: S3 Week 9 - Flex / Clinical Remediation
templates.review_notes: Content determined by mid-semester data — FTO feedback, webapp completion gaps, instructor observations. Decision made by end of Week 7. This is intentionally open.

stations.description: 5 rhythms — focus on specific rhythms students are missing on internship per FTO feedback data.

Potential uses: targeted skill remediation based on FTO feedback, drill card makeup for incomplete students, focused rhythm review if EKG gaps persist, protocol review for identified weak areas, additional minicode practice if progression needs more time at Pressured phase.

tracking.note: Log whatever is completed during flex week

stations.description: Log all completions and remediation work. Preview Weeks 10-13 expectations.
5 rhythm strips, timed. ID rhythm + state treatment. Focus on rhythms students have been weakest on.
equipment: Printed rhythm strips (5 selected based on weak areas)

stations.description: Today: pediatric medication accuracy + Full Lead minicode. Assign groups to stations.

Individual station. Student receives pediatric scenario card, identifies Broselow color, calculates weight-based dose, draws medication, demonstrates 3-way stopcock technique, verbalizes dose confirmation. 8-12 min per student. Rotate through 2-3 cards.
equipment: 3-way stopcock assembly with IV tubing and flush syringe
Mock medication vials (epi 1:10000, atropine, amiodarone, D10, adenosine)

stations.station_name: Minicode — Pediatric Cardiac Arrest (Full Lead Phase)
stations.description: Pediatric cardiac arrest minicode. Full Lead phase — Team Lead runs the code independently, no coaching. Instructor observes as FTO. Debrief after each rotation.
Key takeaways. Log Peds Med Draw completions and minicode participation. Preview Weeks 11-13 expectations.

templates.title: S3 Week 11 - Capstone Scenario Block
templates.review_notes: Select 3-4 capstone scenarios based on cohort weak areas. Each should include a 12-lead component and oral defense questions.

stations.description: 5 rhythms — full treatment pathway verbalization including drug names, doses, and decision points. Integrated application phase.
Capstone scenarios today. Full Lead: you run the call, instructor observes. Oral defense questions after each scenario. Assign first team.

3-4 complex scenarios, 12-15 min each (including debrief). Team Lead rotates each scenario. Full Lead phase — no coaching. 2-3 oral defense questions after each scenario directed at Team Lead. Every student gets at least 1 Team Lead rotation.
equipment: IV/IO kit, drug kit, airway equipment

instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 1 runs scenario (presents case, controls deterioration), 1 acts as FTO/evaluator (observes Team Lead, documents, leads oral defense)
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: Run scenarios sequentially with full group. Solo instructor runs and evaluates. Observers identify what went right and what they'd do differently.

stations.description: Themes from capstone scenarios. Common errors across all runs. Preview: Weeks 12-13 are 4-hour extended blocks — come ready to work. Log all scenario participation and roles.

templates.title: S3 Week 12 - Internship Prep I (Extended Block)
templates.review_notes: Pull webapp completion data: who still has gaps? Prepare repeat EKG assessment strips (same 20 core rhythms as Week 2 baseline). Select 4-6 capstone scenarios. Print protocol case card sets or prepare protocol books with chief complaint cards.

stations.station_name: Rhythm Speed Drill / Repeat EKG Assessment
stations.description: Full group: 20 rhythm strips, timed. This doubles as the repeat EKG assessment — use same strip set as Week 2 baseline. Score per-rhythm for comparison.
Assign Group A (~10 students) and Group B (~10 students). Explain rotation: Group A starts with scenarios, Group B starts with protocol review + skills. Groups swap at midpoint.

stations.station_name: Block 1 — Capstone Scenarios (Group A) / Protocol + Skills (Group B)
stations.description: GROUP A: Capstone scenarios with oral defense. 2-3 scenarios, rotating Team Lead. Full Lead — no coaching. || GROUP B: Protocol review case cards by category (Cardiac → Respiratory → Trauma → Medical → Pediatric → Toxicology) with self-check answer keys. Drill card makeup for incomplete students. Individual megacode rotations if instructor available.
equipment: Full scenario setup for Group A
Protocol case cards or protocol books + chief complaint cards for Group B
Drill card equipment for makeup stations

instructor_tiering.three_instructors: 1-2 run scenarios, 1 facilitates protocol review + skill makeup for Group B
instructor_tiering.two_instructors: 1 runs scenario, 1 rotates between FTO role and protocol review oversight
instructor_tiering.one_instructor: Run scenarios sequentially; Group B works protocol case cards with self-check answer keys independently

tracking.note: Log scenario participation for Group A, station completions for Group B makeup, protocol categories reviewed

stations.description: Full group break. Groups prepare to swap.

GROUP B (now): Capstone scenarios — different scenarios if possible. || GROUP A (now): Protocol case cards (different category sets), megacode rotations, skill makeup. Every student gets scenario time AND protocol review time.

tracking.note: Continue logging all completions and scenario participation

stations.description: Full group debrief. Common themes. SNHD protocol test reminders. Preview Week 13: last lab before OSCE. Log ALL completions.

templates.title: S3 Week 13 - Internship Prep II (Extended Block)
templates.review_notes: LAST LAB BEFORE OSCE. Pull all completion data. Compare Week 2 baseline EKG to Week 12 repeat. Identify students with remaining gaps. Prepare OSCE expectations brief.

stations.description: Full group: 15-20 strips, rapid fire. Goal: identify each rhythm in <10 seconds. Confidence builder, not formal assessment.
Last lab before OSCE. Set the tone. Assign groups A and B.

stations.station_name: Block 1 — Scenario Marathon (Group A) / Protocol + Final Makeup (Group B)
stations.description: GROUP A: Back-to-back capstone scenarios with minimal reset time. Extended oral defense questioning — push deeper: 'What's your differential? Why not X? What would you tell the receiving physician?' Every student MUST get a Team Lead rotation today. || GROUP B: Focus on uncompleted protocol categories. Final drill card makeup. Students who scored poorly on repeat EKG work through weak rhythms with strips and answer keys.

tracking.note: Log everything — this is the final data capture before OSCE

stations.description: Break. Groups swap.

Groups swap. Fresh scenarios if available. Continue oral defense intensity. Push through remaining protocol and skill gaps. Students who have completed everything can pair up and quiz each other using Hot Seat format.

FULL GROUP: What to expect at OSCE — single complex scenario + structured oral board with Medical Director(s). Evaluation areas: clinical reasoning, prioritization, intervention accuracy, communication, stress performance. How to prepare between now and Week 14: review protocols, practice rhythm ID, think through decisions out loud. Q&A. Address anxiety. Reinforce: if you've been doing the work in lab, you're prepared. END-OF-DAY DATA CHECK: verify all drill card completions, all students have had Team Lead rotation in Weeks 11-13, EKG repeat assessment logged, flag students with significant gaps for Medical Directors.
equipment: OSCE format briefing notes (from OSCE planning chat)

templates.title: S3 Week 14 - OSCE Review Board
templates.review_notes: Format and logistics determined by OSCE planning chat. Medical Director availability drives scheduling. Webapp completion data can be shared with Medical Directors as context for oral board questioning.

stations.station_name: OSCE — Single Complex Scenario + Structured Oral Board
stations.description: Single complex medical scenario with psychomotor integration followed by structured post-scenario oral board. Evaluation domains: clinical reasoning, prioritization, intervention accuracy, communication clarity, stress performance. Observers: Medical Director(s), program leadership, instructors. Detailed plan from OSCE planning chat.

tracking.note: Grading rubric and evaluation tool from OSCE planning chat
